And so giving the clinical success of HIF-2 alpha inhibitors, especially belzutifan recently, we wanted to test NEO811 and belzutifan in combination... not only adding a HIF-2 alpha inhibitor with ARNT, so they're targeting the exact same pathway, is that hopefully in the future, this will open us up to other combinations of other standards of care.

description: While CCNE1 amplification is a strong biomarker, some amplified cell lines show reduced sensitivity, potentially due to P16 expression or other factors.
transcript_evidence: Yes. So one interesting phenomenon is that cyclin E1 amplified cancers are almost always P16 high, so they express an endogenous inhibitor of CDK4/6. So it appears that, and there's also evidence for that, that there is a downside of having both hyperactive CDK2 and active CDK4/6 on top of it. So it's either/or in most cases. There was, I think in this panel, two exceptions, which had low P16, and this explains that then CDK4/6 is active at the same time as CDK2 and pure cyclin E1 degradation doesn't work.
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the interplay of CCNE1 amplification, P16 expression, and CDK4/6 activity to refine patient stratification for MRT55811.

transcript_evidence: So to test that in BT474 cell lines, which is a HER2-amplified model, we gave the combined approach of 10203 in combination with tucatinib here on the left or trastuzumab here on the right. And what you can see in blue is strong monotherapy effects with 10203. But when you combine that with the other agent, it led to strong regressions... And when given in combination with the HER2 targeting antibody trastuzumab, led to strong regressions, and again, outperformed the combination of tucatinib plus trastuzumab.
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the synergistic effects of BB010203 with HER2-targeted agents and other therapies in HER2-amplified cancers to optimize combination strategies.

description: The disruption of non-canonical RAS signaling by BB010203 may address resistance mechanisms to canonical RAS inhibitors and HER2-targeted therapies.
transcript_evidence: So this data shows by genetic mechanisms that the PIK3CA RAS interaction is essential for phospho-AKT activation and survival. So to understand what RAS isoforms were driving this dependency, the first thing we did is we screened our panel of our two amplified lines against with 10203 compared to the pan-RAS inhibitor 6236. And what we observed is that with 10203, as expected, we saw complete phospho-AKT suppression. But with the pan-RAS inhibitor RMC-6236, we saw moderate suppression of phospho-AKT, despite seeing complete phospho-ERK suppression. This was seen across a larger panel of lines, here shown on the right. So this data to us indicated that the phospho-AKT activity in these lines was not likely driven from canonical RAS proteins, which were inhibited by the RMC-6236, but likely coming from non-canonical proteins, which were not hit by 6236.
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify resistance mechanisms to HER2-targeted therapies and canonical RAS inhibitors that are driven by non-canonical RAS signaling, and assess BB010203's potential to overcome them.

But additionally, what you can see also is that we do extend the tumor growth delay in a dose-dependent manner when we add our KAT2 inhibitor with the bispecific antibody. And this is reflected in the survival curve shown on the right. So we're very excited about the idea that this could potentially open up opportunities to combine our KAT2 inhibitor with IO therapies to deepen responses in multiple myeloma.
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can analyze the immune-modulatory effects of KAT2 inhibitors and identify synergistic combinations with T-cell engagers and other immunotherapies.

transcript_evidence: So we combined our KAT2 inhibitor with either lenalidomide or pomalidomide in a combination matrix experiment for proliferation in vitro. ... So when you get this output data, if you get a value between zero to one, this is considered additive, and you can see this by the column on the right where we have the self-cross of our compound with itself and those values range there. If you have a value that's greater than one, this suggests you can have synergy between the two compounds, and you can see for both our KAT2 inhibitor combination with lenalidomide and pomalidomide, most of the cell lines we observe synergistic scores.
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify patient populations with multiple myeloma who may benefit from combination therapy with KAT2 inhibitors and IMiDs.

description: RMO-55 demonstrates activity in models of acquired resistance to RAS on inhibitors, particularly those involving KRAS amplification or increased expression.
transcript_evidence: So going back to the clinical resistance I started with, one of the major mechanisms of clinical resistance, at least in terms of where we can detect a mechanism of resistance from genomic alterations in ctDNA, is KRAS amplification of the mutant associated with the G12 mutant allele... However, RMO-55 at 10 MPK was able to produce deep and very durable regressions in these resistant models. In a related model, we looked at the ability of RMO-55 to restore tumor control in tumors that were already outgrowing on deraxone rasib. And you can see here, these tumors initially showed regression, but then started to grow out with continued deraxone rasib treatment. However, in the group that we switched over to RMO-55, we were able to restore tumor control and had durable regressions out to the end of the study.
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can identify resistance mechanisms to RAS inhibitors, including those involving RAS amplification or overexpression, and assess RMO-55's potential to overcome them.

A very nice talk. So you show that the RM-055 has stimulate the GTP hydrolysis compared to daxonesib. So compared to direct combo with the RAS-off inhibitor, does this RM-055 provide the advantage for the combo with the RAS-off inhibitor? description: RMO-55 represents a new class of RAS inhibitor with a catalytic mechanism, offering a distinct approach compared to existing RAS on and RAS off inhibitors.
transcript_evidence: Thus far, there's really been two major classes of RAS inhibitor. The first class is the RAS off inhibitors. These are switch two pocket binders... The other class of inhibitors is the molecular glues or tricomplex inhibitors. These bind exclusively to the on state of RAS... Today, I would like to introduce a third class of inhibitors. Oh, my goodness. That basically catalytically inactivate RAS and then essentially stimulate the hydrolysis of RAS GTP to RAS GDP and mutant RAS, and in so doing, mimic the activity of natural gaps at wild-type RAS.
crispro_angle: CrisPRO can highlight the unique catalytic mechanism of RMO-55 and its potential advantages over existing RAS inhibitor classes in addressing resistance and unmet needs.
